title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Language Clinic: Main Boundary
summary: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the main boundary increment in testable command lifecycle and function map. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-main-boundary — Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_cli_lifecycle_contract_3(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-main-boundary-xzcerm
# Competency: bookbot-main-boundary.
# Case 3586: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_main_boundary_xzcerm(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xzcerm = "xzcerm-mreczx-3586"
    assert evidence_variant_xzcerm
    evidence_axes_xzcerm = (
        "corpusxzcerm",
        "policymreczx",
        "revision3586",
        "commandxzcerm3586",
        "reportmreczx3586",
        "test3586xzcerm",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xzcerm)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, keep Main Boundary connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Main Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 1704 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Code at module top level is harmless because it runs only from the command line.
